stimulus: Many bakeries have begun offering gluten-free versions of traditional pastries in recent years, but ______blank Food scientist Renata Velasquez and her colleagues designed a study to find out.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice D is the best answer. The convention being tested is end-of-sentence punctuation. This choice correctly uses a question mark to punctuate the interrogative clause "do customers actually enjoy these alternatives," which asks a direct question at the end of the sentence. This is the question that the researchers in the next sentence were trying to investigate.

Choice A is incorrect because the context requires an interrogative clause, not a declarative one. Choice B is incorrect because a period can't be used to punctuate an interrogative clause. Choice C is incorrect because a question mark can't be used to punctuate a declarative clause.
